title: Life: A Mystery — I
desc: Part of a triptych depicting squirrels navigating a world reshaped by human advancement — small creatures as symbols of ecological fragility.
hover: Squirrel on a sunflower — life in harmony with nature, now increasingly threatened by human progress.

title: Life: A Mystery — II
desc: Modern technologies and urbanization have made these small creatures increasingly vulnerable — a quiet protest through fine art.
hover: A squirrel caught between electric wires — the clash between wildlife and modern infrastructure.

title: Life: A Mystery — III
desc: The third in the series — squirrels inadvertently aid the growth of trees, contributing to ecological balance now under threat.
hover: Squirrels as seed-planters — their role in maintaining ecological balance, now endangered by habitat loss.

title: Subconscious Muse
desc: A light bulb symbolizes power at the center — two faces of positivity and negativity — with a chair representing the dynamic, shifting nature of inner power.
hover: Two faces flanking a glowing light bulb of power — positivity and negativity in eternal balance, guided by the subconscious mind.

title: Reflection of Nature — I
desc: A salt-textured surface contrasting ancient and modern human faces — how progress has led to both advancement and environmental harm.
hover: First circular canvas — texture created by sprinkling salt, contrasting an elderly face from the past with modern progress.

title: Reflection of Nature — II
desc: A bird symbolizing nature's hidden power to restore balance — even through destruction, the natural world reclaims itself.
hover: Second circular canvas — a bird with spread wings portraying nature's beauty and its exploitation.

title: Colour Obsession with Music
desc: A paint tube as the artist sits before an easel — music visually expressed on the canvas, symbolizing the emotional attachment artists feel to sound during creation.
hover: A paint tube sitting on a chair as the artist — the canvas on the easel visually expresses music.

title: Wings of Notion
desc: The artist symbolized as a free bird painting in a bird's nest — a blue palette dripping from a cloud-like tube highlights the deep bond between creativity and nature.
hover: The artist as a free bird, painting in a nest — paint tubes emerging from eggs like children.

title: Pacific Warrior
desc: A peaceful warrior who battles inner turmoil through art — the calm natural setting reflects the resolution of inner conflict without violence.
hover: The artist as a peaceful warrior — the brush as weapon, inner turmoil resolved through art not violence.

title: Musical Palette
desc: The artist's connection between music and painting — a palette resembling a violin, a galaxy background symbolizing the artist's boundless creativity during creation.
hover: The brush and palette resembling a violin creating melody — boundless creativity immersed in music.

title: Primaries of Nature
desc: Three small paintings depicting primary color tubes connected to nature — blue for water, yellow for soil, red for fire — symbolizing nature's core elements.
hover: Three natural color tubes — blue for water, yellow for soil, red for fire and the sun.

title: Waiting (Moments of Waiting)
desc: Ancient and modern times contrasted — despite advancements in communication, the experience of waiting still exists. Waiting may have reduced, but it remains eternal.
hover: Past and present side by side — a letter and a bird versus a modern device — yet waiting remains eternal.

title: Puzzle
desc: A lotus and lamp at the center symbolize hope remaining in the relationship — a rope as the bond between people, growing tangled when not cared for through communication.
hover: A rope — the bond between two people — if pulled gently stays steady, but twisted it tangles and ruins the relationship.

title: Nostalgia
desc: A mirror as emotional confinement — blue symbolizes the mind's darkness and the haunting weight of memories that anchor someone in the past, making it hard to move on.
hover: A person chained to a mirror — trapped in memory, the blue of the mind's darkness weighing heavily.

title: Still There Is a Hope
desc: A colour palette shaped like a lotus leaf divided into four levels of challenging life phases — a final lotus flower with a candle symbolizes enduring hope.
hover: A palette shaped as a lotus leaf — four levels of life's challenges — at the summit, a lotus with a candle: hope always remains.

title: Fear of Blooming
desc: The hesitation that hinders personal growth — a woman in white, surrounded by a dark flower with watching eyes, afraid to move forward for fear of being hurt again.
hover: A woman in white at the center — graceful yet afraid to bloom — surrounded by a dark flower with watching eyes.

title: Inner Conflict
desc: Multiple colourful doors represent the dilemma of choosing the right path — a woman on a lotus holds a key, but recognizing the right door remains a great challenge.
hover: Multiple colourful doors represent the dilemma of choosing the right path — a woman on a lotus holds the key.

title: Expectations
desc: People have many expectations of you — yet it is not necessary to meet all of them. This painting depicts the burden of expectations and encourages breaking free to prioritize personal growth.
hover: The burden of expectations — encouraging breaking free from those bonds to prioritize one's own happiness.

title: Vigilance
desc: A surrealist exploration — at every moment, either you are under someone's protection or, against your will, someone is watching you within an inescapable boundary.
hover: Circles of vigilance — always within a boundary you cannot escape, no matter how much you try.

title: Immersion (Tanmayata)
desc: Tanmayata — to be completely absorbed in something. A human face with light pink shades representing the dual aspects of concentration that both disrupt and enhance deep absorption.
hover: To be completely absorbed — a state where attention is fully focused, separating one from the surrounding world.

title: Mahakumbh
desc: Created for Mahakumbh 2025 — the mythology of Samudra Manthan at the center, crowned by the symbol of Satyamev Jayate, representing the form of entire India.
hover: Mahakumbh 2025 — the mythology of Samudra Manthan and the symbol of Satyamev Jayate.

title: Emotion Series — I
desc: A series of watercolour studies expressing different emotions — personal experiences and growth, the transparent quality of watercolour reflecting the vulnerability of feeling.
hover: Inner feelings expressed in watercolour — personal experiences, growth, and the transparent flow of emotion.

title: Emotion Series — II
desc: Emotions expressed through transparent watercolour strips — relationships and personal growth observed through the spontaneous, luminous quality of the medium.
hover: Observing the flow of emotions — keeping feelings in one place and watching them move through transparent washes.

title: Emotion Series — III
desc: Painted in strips using watercolour, showing the transparent quality — different emotions related to personal experiences, relationships, and growth.
hover: The third in the emotion watercolour series — capturing a mood, a moment, a feeling too complex for words.
